slides.title: Accessibility and Distribution of Urban Green Spaces
slides.subtitle: A Spatial Analysis of Kedamay Weyane Sub-City

slides.title: Introduction
slides.content: Urban green spaces play a crucial role in improving environmental quality, public health, and urban livability. However, rapid urbanization often leads to unequal distribution and limited accessibility of these spaces.
bullets: Green spaces improve urban environmental quality
Urban growth often reduces available green areas

slides.title: Problem Statement
slides.content: Many rapidly growing cities experience uneven distribution of green spaces. Some neighborhoods have good access to parks while others lack adequate green areas.
bullets: Urban expansion reduces available green areas
Need for spatial analysis to support planning decisions

slides.title: Research Objectives
slides.content: The study aims to evaluate the distribution and accessibility of urban green spaces.
bullets: Assess the spatial distribution of green spaces
Identify areas with limited green space access

slides.title: Research Questions
bullets: How are green spaces distributed within the sub-city?
How accessible are these green spaces for residents?
Which areas lack adequate access to green spaces?
How can planning improve equitable distribution?

slides.title: Study Area
slides.content: The research focuses on Kedamay Weyane Sub-City, located in Mekelle, Ethiopia. The area is an important residential and service zone experiencing rapid urban development.
bullets: Growing demand for public green spaces

slides.title: Literature Insight
bullets: Urban green spaces improve environmental sustainability
Accessibility is essential for equitable urban development
WHO recommends minimum green space per capita
Planning policies should ensure fair distribution

slides.title: Case Study: Addis Ababa
slides.content: The Addis Ababa case study shows that rapid urbanization has reduced green spaces and created unequal distribution across the city.
bullets: Green spaces concentrated in wealthier districts
Per capita green space below recommended standards

slides.title: Case Study: Johannesburg
slides.content: The Johannesburg case study highlights spatial inequality in green space distribution due to historical planning patterns.
bullets: Green spaces concentrated in high-income areas
Planning policies aim to reduce inequality
Shows importance of equitable green space planning

slides.title: Case Study Comparison
right_points: Green spaces concentrated in rich areas

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: Urban green spaces are essential for sustainable and livable cities. Understanding their spatial distribution and accessibility helps planners create healthier and more equitable urban environments.
bullets: Green spaces improve quality of life
Accessibility must be considered in planning

slides.quote: Cities are not just buildings and roads, they are spaces for people to live, interact, and thrive.
slides.author: Urban Planning Principle
